\\n        return optim.lr_scheduler.LinearLR(optimizer, start_factor=0.3, end_factor=1.0, total_iters=max(total_steps, 1))\\n    warmup = optim.lr_scheduler.LinearLR(optimizer, start_factor=0.3, end_factor=1.0, total_iters=warmup_steps)\\n    cosine = optim.lr_scheduler.CosineAnnealingLR(optimizer, T_max=total_steps - warmup_steps)\\n    return optim.lr_scheduler.SequentialLR(optimizer, schedulers=[warmup, cosine], milestones=[warmup_steps])\",\"text\":\"\"}],\"primary_mode\":\"summaries\",\"rationale\":\"Use the fixed 256-step budget more effectively by making the learning-rate schedule match the actual verifier horizon instead of an implicit 100-epoch run.
